chapter_summary: Nicodemus comes to Jesus at night; Jesus explains being 'born again' by the Spirit; John 3:16 — the most famous verse in the Bible; John the Baptist rejoices that Jesus must increase.
main_lesson: God loves the world so much that He sent Jesus so that whoever believes in Him will live forever.
memory_verse: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life. (John 3:16)
parent_guide: John 3:16 is the heart of the gospel. Nicodemus was a religious leader who came at night — probably afraid to be seen. Jesus tells him that new life requires being 'born of the Spirit.' v16 is the summary of the whole Bible in one verse. Take your time here.
application_for_children: God loves YOU so much that He sent Jesus. If you believe in Jesus, you will live with God forever.

verses.kjv: There was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:
verses.little_bible: There was a man named Nicodemus. He was a Pharisee — a religious teacher — and a very important leader among the Jewish people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man named Nicodemus was an important religious teacher.
verses.meaning: Nicodemus was highly educated, respected, and religious. Yet he still needed Jesus. Status and knowledge are not enough.
verses.memory_phrase: Even important people need Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, no matter how much I know or how good I try to be, I need You.
verses.discussion_question: Nicodemus was an important, educated man. Do you think religious people need Jesus just as much as everyone else?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we think if we go to church and know the Bible, we have everything we need. What did Nicodemus show us?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone who seems to have everything together — and remember that everyone needs Jesus.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, pray for someone you know who is religious but may not yet have a personal relationship with Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dignified older man in fine religious robes, carrying a scroll, standing at the edge of a moonlit courtyard. Authority and quiet uncertainty on his face.

verses.kjv: The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him.
verses.little_bible: Nicodemus came to Jesus at night. He said: 'Teacher, we know that God sent You — no one could do the miracles You do unless God is with them.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nicodemus came to Jesus at night and said: 'God must be with You because of all the miracles You do.'
verses.meaning: Nicodemus came at night — secretly, perhaps afraid. But he came. He acknowledged that Jesus was from God. This was the beginning of his journey.
verses.memory_phrase: Nicodemus came to Jesus in the dark.
verses.prayer: Jesus, even when I come to You unsure or afraid, You are there. Thank You for receiving me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Nicodemus came at night instead of in the day?
verses.family_discussion: Nicodemus took a risk coming to Jesus. Is there a step of faith you know you need to take but have been putting off?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus today with something you have been afraid to bring — a question, a doubt, a need.
verses.action_challenge: Write down one honest question you have about God and look for the answer together this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older man in fine robes walking through dark streets toward a glowing lamp where Jesus sits. Night sky, shadows, one warm light ahead.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to him: 'I tell you the truth — unless a person is born again, they cannot even see the kingdom of God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You must be born again to see God's kingdom.'
verses.meaning: Jesus cuts straight to the real need — not information but transformation. Being born again is not improvement; it is new life from God.
verses.memory_phrase: You must be born again.
verses.prayer: God, give me new life — true life — from You. Born again by Your Spirit.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'born again' means? Can you be born twice?
verses.family_discussion: Being born again means God gives you a completely new life on the inside. Has there been a moment in your family's story when someone was born again?
verses.do_it_today: Ask yourself honestly: 'Have I been born again — has God given me new life on the inside?'
verses.action_challenge: If a family member has a testimony of being born again, ask them to share the story with the whole family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking to Nicodemus in lamplight, hand raised in emphasis. The word 'again' shown visually as a fresh seed sprouting from worn soil. Night setting, warm lamp glow.

verses.kjv: Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother's womb, and be born?
verses.little_bible: Nicodemus was confused. He asked: 'How can someone be born when they are old? How can they go back into their mother's womb and be born again?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nicodemus was confused. He asked: 'How can anyone be born twice?'
verses.meaning: Nicodemus heard Jesus literally. He was thinking physically when Jesus was speaking spiritually. Spiritual truth can only be understood with the Spirit's help.
verses.memory_phrase: Nicodemus did not understand yet.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, help me understand the things Jesus says that are hard to grasp.
verses.discussion_question: Nicodemus was a smart man — but he missed what Jesus was saying. Can being smart sometimes get in the way of understanding God?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we try to figure out spiritual things with our minds alone. How does the Holy Spirit help us understand the Bible?
verses.do_it_today: Before reading the Bible today, ask the Holy Spirit: 'Help me understand what God is saying.'
verses.action_challenge: Make it a family habit to pray before reading the Bible — asking the Holy Spirit to help you understand.
verses.illustration_prompt: Nicodemus with a puzzled expression, gesturing with his hands, brow furrowed. Jesus watching him patiently, waiting to explain further. Warm indoor lamplight.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'I tell you truly — unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, they cannot enter the kingdom of God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You need to be born of water and the Holy Spirit to enter God's kingdom.'
verses.meaning: Water may refer to physical birth or to baptism. The Spirit is the new birth God gives. Both the outward (water) and the inward (Spirit) matter.
verses.memory_phrase: Born of water and the Spirit.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, make me new on the inside. I want the kind of life only You can give.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between being born physically and being born spiritually?
verses.family_discussion: We were all born physically — but when were we born spiritually? Has each person in our family been born of the Spirit?
verses.do_it_today: If you have never asked the Holy Spirit to give you new life, you can do it right now.
verses.action_challenge: Discuss what it means to be born of the Spirit. If any family member has questions about how to receive new life, answer them honestly and pray together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two images side by side: a baby being born in warm light, and a gentle dove descending with glowing light — physical and spiritual birth. Warm, tender tones.

verses.kjv: That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
verses.little_bible: What is born from a human body is human — physical. But what is born from God's Spirit is spiritual — eternal.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: What is born from our bodies is human. What is born from God's Spirit is spiritual.
verses.meaning: Physical birth gives physical life. Spiritual birth gives spiritual life. We need both — but only the Spirit can give the eternal kind.
verses.memory_phrase: What is born of the Spirit is spirit.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for the life my parents gave me. And thank You for the life only You can give me.
verses.discussion_question: Can a person have physical life but not spiritual life? What is missing in that case?
verses.family_discussion: Physical life is precious — God gave it. But spiritual life is eternal. How does our family nurture both?
verses.do_it_today: Do something to nourish your spirit today — read the Bible, pray, worship, or just sit quietly with God.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, talk about the difference between caring for the body and caring for the spirit. Make a plan to invest in both.
verses.illustration_prompt: Split image: a human figure made of warm, earthy colours on one side; the same figure filled with luminous spiritual light on the other. Contrast of natural and supernatural.

verses.kjv: Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Don't be so surprised that I told you — you must be born again.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Do not be surprised. You must be born again.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was not backing down from this statement. Being born again is not optional, surprising, or for certain kinds of people — it is necessary for all.
verses.memory_phrase: You must be born again — Jesus said so.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I do not want to be surprised or resistant. I want the new life You are offering.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus said 'marvel not' — don't be surprised? Was this a strange thing to say?
verses.family_discussion: The word 'must' is important. Jesus did not say 'it would be nice if.' He said you must. How seriously do we take that?
verses.do_it_today: Let the words 'You must be born again' settle in your heart today. What is your response?
verses.action_challenge: Read John 3:3-7 together slowly as a family. Stop after each verse and ask: 'What is Jesus saying?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a calm, firm expression — speaking directly into the eyes of Nicodemus. No drama, just truth. Warm lamplight between them.

verses.kjv: The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The wind blows where it wants to. You can hear it, but you cannot see where it comes from or where it goes. It is the same with everyone born of the Spirit.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said the Holy Spirit is like wind — you cannot see it, but you can feel what it does.
verses.meaning: The Holy Spirit moves freely, mysteriously, and powerfully — like wind. We cannot control Him, but we can experience His work.
verses.memory_phrase: The Spirit is like the wind — you cannot see it, but you feel it.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, blow through my life like wind — bringing life and change.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt the wind but not been able to see it? How is the Holy Spirit like wind?
verses.family_discussion: The Holy Spirit cannot be controlled or predicted — He moves where He wants. How does that make you feel about God?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and feel the wind. Remember: the Holy Spirit is real, powerful, and moving — even when you cannot see Him.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, pray and ask the Holy Spirit to 'blow through' your home and your lives this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Trees bending in a gentle breeze, leaves swirling. A figure with eyes closed, feeling the wind on their face. The invisible presence made tangible. Peaceful and alive.

verses.kjv: Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?
verses.little_bible: Nicodemus asked: 'How is any of this possible?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nicodemus still asked: 'How can this be?'
verses.meaning: Nicodemus was still trying to understand with his mind alone. His question is honest — and Jesus did not dismiss it.
verses.memory_phrase: Nicodemus kept asking and Jesus kept answering.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I have questions too. Thank You that You are patient with me.
verses.discussion_question: Nicodemus kept asking questions. Is it okay to ask questions about God?
verses.family_discussion: What questions does your family have about God? Take one and explore it together this week.
verses.do_it_today: Write down one honest question you have about God or the Bible. Start looking for the answer.
verses.action_challenge: Keep a family 'Big Questions' journal — write your questions and answers as you discover them over time.
verses.illustration_prompt: Nicodemus with hands open in a gesture of genuine searching. Jesus listens with patience and warmth. A night sky visible through a window — questions and stars.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You are a teacher in Israel — and you do not understand these things?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You are a teacher in Israel and you do not understand this?'
verses.meaning: Jesus challenged Nicodemus gently. The Old Testament pointed to all of this — the Spirit's work, new life, transformation. Nicodemus should have seen it.
verses.memory_phrase: Knowing the Bible is not the same as knowing God.
verses.prayer: God, help me not just to know Your Word in my head but to know You in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Nicodemus was a Bible teacher but still missed something. What do you think he was missing?
verses.family_discussion: We can know a lot about God without truly knowing God. What is the difference?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to move what you know in your head about Him into your heart — so it becomes real.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, talk about the difference between 'head knowledge' and 'heart knowledge' of God. Which are you pursuing?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing to a scroll in Nicodemus's hand, then pointing to his own heart. A gentle challenge. The scroll is not enough.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I tell you truly — we speak about what we know and testify to what we have seen. But you people do not accept our testimony.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'We speak what we know — but people do not receive it.'
verses.meaning: Jesus speaks with firsthand knowledge — He has been with the Father. His testimony is absolutely reliable. The tragedy is that people refuse to receive it.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus speaks what He knows.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I receive Your words. I believe You know exactly what You are talking about.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said He speaks from what He knows. How is that different from someone who just gives opinions?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus speaks with complete authority because He was there — in heaven — before He came to earth. How does that increase our trust in His words?
verses.do_it_today: Read one saying of Jesus today with this thought: 'He knows this is true. He has seen it.'
verses.action_challenge: Together, find a teaching of Jesus that seems hard to believe. Discuss: 'What would it mean to truly trust what Jesus says here?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with calm, firsthand certainty — eyes clear, hands open. Nicodemus listening but uncertain. Truth being offered, not yet fully received.

verses.kjv: If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'If you do not believe Me when I talk about things you can see and understand — how will you ever believe Me when I talk about heavenly things?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'If you do not believe the simple things, how will you believe the heavenly things?'
verses.meaning: Jesus is leading Nicodemus from the familiar to the eternal. Faith must grow — starting with what Jesus says about what we can observe.
verses.memory_phrase: Start by trusting Jesus in the small things.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me trust You in small, everyday things — so that my faith grows for bigger things.
verses.discussion_question: Why does trusting Jesus with small things help us trust Him with bigger things?
verses.family_discussion: Has our family seen God be faithful in small things? How does that build faith for bigger things?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one small thing today and consciously trust God with it — just that one thing.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, share stories of God being faithful in small things. Let those stories build your faith for bigger prayers.
verses.illustration_prompt: A stone pathway leading from a simple village up to a mountain with clouds at the top. Each step lit by warm sunlight. Growing faith visualised as a journey upward.

verses.kjv: And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man which is in heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'No one has ever gone up to heaven except the One who came down from heaven — the Son of Man, who is in heaven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came from heaven. He is the only One who knows what heaven is like.
verses.meaning: Jesus speaks of heavenly things with authority because He came from heaven. He is the only reliable witness of what is above.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came from heaven.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You left heaven to come to me. That is the most amazing thing I can imagine. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus came from heaven. What does that tell us about who He really is?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave up heaven to come to earth for us. When we think about the cross, we should start here — with how much He gave up.
verses.do_it_today: Spend a moment marvelling at this: Jesus left heaven. For you. Right now.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, imagine what heaven might be like. Then talk about what it cost Jesus to leave it for us.
verses.illustration_prompt: A radiant figure descending from a glowing heaven above through clouds to a dark earth below. Light from above illuminating everything below. Wonder and sacrifice.

verses.kjv: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Just as Moses lifted up a bronze snake in the wilderness for the people to look at and be saved, I must also be lifted up.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Just like Moses lifted up the snake, I must be lifted up too.'
verses.meaning: Jesus pointed to Numbers 21 — when Israel was dying from snake bites, Moses lifted a bronze serpent and those who looked at it were healed. Jesus would be 'lifted up' on the cross — and all who look to Him in faith will be saved.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus must be lifted up so we can be saved.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I look to You — lifted up on the cross — and I trust that You save me.
verses.discussion_question: In the Old Testament, the people who looked at the bronze snake were healed. What do we need to look at to be saved?
verses.family_discussion: Looking to Jesus is what saves us — not trying harder, not being good enough, not doing enough. Just looking. How does that make you feel?
verses.do_it_today: Look at a cross today — in your home, in a church, on a picture. Let it remind you of what Jesus did.
verses.action_challenge: Read Numbers 21:4-9 as a family. Then talk about how it points to Jesus on the cross.
verses.illustration_prompt: Moses in the wilderness holding up a pole with a bronze serpent, people looking up at it. Then an echo of that image with a cross on a hill. Connection across the ages.

verses.kjv: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
verses.little_bible: So that everyone who believes in Him will not die — but will have eternal life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Anyone who believes in Jesus will live forever.
verses.meaning: The first mention of eternal life in this conversation. The condition is belief — trusting in Jesus who was lifted up.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in Jesus and have eternal life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. Thank You that eternal life is Your gift to me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'believe' in Jesus? Is it just knowing facts about Him?
verses.family_discussion: Eternal life starts now — not just after we die. How does knowing we have eternal life change how we live today?
verses.do_it_today: Say today: 'I believe in Jesus. I have eternal life.' Say it as a statement of faith.
verses.action_challenge: Talk about what eternal life means as a family — not just length of life but the quality of life in relationship with God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure looking upward at a cross on a hill, light streaming down from it. Chains falling off their hands. Hope and freedom in the image.

verses.kjv: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
verses.little_bible: God loves every person in the world SO much that He gave His only Son, Jesus. Anyone who believes in Jesus will not perish but will live forever with God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God loves you so much He sent Jesus. If you trust in Jesus, you will live with God forever.
verses.meaning: This is the heart of the entire Bible in one verse. God's love was the motivation; giving Jesus was the action; belief is the response; everlasting life is the result. Every word carries weight.
verses.memory_phrase: God so loved the world that He gave His Son.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for loving the world — and loving me — SO much that You gave Jesus. I believe in Him. I want to live with You forever.
verses.discussion_question: God gave His only Son — that is the most precious gift anyone could give. What does that tell you about how much He loves you?
verses.family_discussion: John 3:16 is the whole gospel in one verse. Can each family member explain it in their own words — what it means, and what it means to them personally?
verses.do_it_today: Memorise John 3:16 today — the whole verse, word for word. It may be the most important verse you ever know.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, write John 3:16 on a card and put it somewhere everyone will see it. Say it together every day for a week.
verses.illustration_prompt: God's hands gently lowering a tiny glowing figure — Jesus — toward a dark earth filled with small figures of people looking up. Overwhelming love and light from above. The single most important moment in history.

verses.kjv: For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.
verses.little_bible: God did not send Jesus into the world to judge and condemn people — He sent Jesus so that the whole world could be saved through Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus did not come to judge us. He came to save us.
verses.meaning: Jesus' first coming was not a court hearing — it was a rescue mission. He came to save, not to condemn. Condemnation is not God's desire for any person.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came to save, not to condemn.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You came for me with open arms — not with a gavel. You came to save me.
verses.discussion_question: Some people are afraid that God is angry with them. What does this verse say about why God sent Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The purpose of Jesus' first coming was rescue. How does knowing that change the way we share the message of Jesus with others?
verses.do_it_today: If you have ever felt condemned or unworthy — read this verse again. This is why Jesus came.
verses.action_challenge: Think of someone who feels far from God, condemned, or unworthy. Pray for them. Then find a way to tell them: Jesus came to save, not to condemn.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with arms wide open, surrounded by people of every kind. No judgment, no distance — only welcome. Warm golden light.

verses.kjv: He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
verses.little_bible: Whoever believes in Jesus is not condemned. But whoever does not believe is already condemned, because they have not trusted in the name of God's only Son.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Whoever believes in Jesus is safe. Whoever does not believe is not safe.
verses.meaning: Condemnation is not something God imposes arbitrarily — it is the natural state of everyone apart from Christ. Belief in Jesus is what removes it.
verses.memory_phrase: Belief in Jesus means no condemnation.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in Your name. Thank You that I am not condemned.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be 'not condemned'? What are we free from when we believe in Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Romans 8:1 says 'There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.' How does your family carry that freedom?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'I believe in Jesus. I am not condemned.' Let that truth sink in.
verses.action_challenge: Look up Romans 8:1 together. Write it out and put it next to John 3:16 on your family verse card.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure stepping out of dark shadows into brilliant light — chains falling off. A cross visible behind them. Freedom and relief on their face.

verses.kjv: And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
verses.little_bible: This is the problem: light has come into the world — but people chose to stay in the darkness because they loved their evil deeds and did not want them exposed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus is the light. But some people prefer the dark because they do not want their wrong things shown up.
verses.meaning: People do not reject Jesus because of lack of evidence — they reject Him because the light exposes what they want to hide. It is a moral problem, not an intellectual one.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the light. Some people choose the dark.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I choose the light. Please show me anything I am hiding from You — and help me bring it into Your light.
verses.discussion_question: Why would someone choose darkness over light? What might they be afraid of?
verses.family_discussion: Coming to the light means letting God see everything — even the things we want to hide. Is there anything our family needs to bring into the light?
verses.do_it_today: Come before God honestly today. Ask Him to show you anything you are hiding — and bring it to His light.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, talk about what it means to 'walk in the light' — being honest, open, and transparent with God and each other.
verses.illustration_prompt: A contrast image: one side dark with shadowy figures hiding; the other side bright with a figure walking toward a warm light. A choice between the two.

verses.kjv: For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.
verses.little_bible: Everyone who does evil hates the light and stays away from it — because they are afraid their evil actions will be exposed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People who do wrong things stay away from Jesus because they do not want to be found out.
verses.meaning: Avoidance of Jesus is often a heart issue — people fear exposure. But the good news is that Jesus does not expose to shame; He exposes to heal.
verses.memory_phrase: Evil runs from the light.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I do not want to run from You. Draw me toward Your light, no matter what it shows.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when you hid something because you did not want to get in trouble? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus sees everything — but He uses that knowledge to heal, not to shame. How can that make it easier to come to Him honestly?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you have been hiding from God? Today is a good day to bring it to Him.
verses.action_challenge: Practice confession as a family — talking to God honestly about wrongs, and receiving His forgiveness freely.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crouching figure in shadows, turning away from a beam of light. The light does not chase them aggressively — it just shines, patiently waiting.

verses.kjv: But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.
verses.little_bible: But whoever lives by the truth comes toward the light gladly — so that it can be seen clearly that what they do is done in God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Those who live honestly come to the light — they are not afraid.
verses.meaning: Walking in truth means welcoming the light. The person who does God's work has nothing to fear from exposure — they want God to be seen in what they do.
verses.memory_phrase: Those who live in truth love the light.
verses.prayer: God, help me live in a way that welcomes Your light. Let everything I do be done for You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'do truth'? Can truth be something you do, not just something you believe?
verses.family_discussion: Do we live in a way that we would be happy for God to see everything — our actions, our words, our motives? What would need to change?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today entirely for God — not for applause or reward. Do it in the light.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, do one act of service or kindness that no one outside the family knows about. Do it purely for God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure walking freely and openly toward a warm light, arms open, face uplifted. Nothing to hide. Peaceful, joyful transparency.

verses.kjv: After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judaea; and there he tarried with them, and baptized.
verses.little_bible: After this, Jesus and His disciples went to the Judean countryside. He spent time there with them, and they were baptising people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus and His disciples went to Judaea and were baptising people there.
verses.meaning: Jesus continued the work John had started — calling people to repentance and baptism. He was not yet done with the wilderness; He kept moving.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus kept doing His work.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to keep doing what You have called me to do — faithfully and quietly.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think baptism was such an important part of what Jesus and His disciples did?
verses.family_discussion: Baptism is an outward sign of an inward change. If your child has been baptised, talk about it. If not, discuss what it would mean.
verses.do_it_today: Continue something God has called you to do today — consistently and faithfully.
verses.action_challenge: Make a list of things your family feels called to do for God. Commit to being faithful in them — like Jesus who kept going.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and His disciples by a river, a group of people wading in. Peaceful countryside. Active, purposeful ministry in an ordinary setting.

verses.kjv: And John also was baptizing in Aenon near to Salim, because there was much water there: and they came, and were baptized.
verses.little_bible: John the Baptist was also still baptising at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there. People kept coming to him and being baptised.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John the Baptist was still baptising people at a place with lots of water.
verses.meaning: Both Jesus and John were active at the same time — the movement was growing. John had not been sidelined yet.
verses.memory_phrase: John and Jesus both kept doing God's work.
verses.prayer: God, help me keep doing what You have called me to, even when others seem to be getting more attention.
verses.discussion_question: Both Jesus and John were doing their work at the same time. Is it okay for different people to do different parts of God's work?
verses.family_discussion: God calls different people to different parts of His mission. How does your family fit into the bigger picture of what God is doing?
verses.do_it_today: Do your part in God's plan today — even if it feels small or unnoticed.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, talk about the specific role each person plays in your family's mission for God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two separate river scenes: one with John baptising in a wide river, another with Jesus doing the same further away. Both busy, both faithful.

verses.kjv: For John was not yet cast into prison.
verses.little_bible: This was happening before John had been put in prison.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This was before John was put in prison.
verses.meaning: A timeline note — this was an earlier, freer season of ministry before John's arrest. It was a window of time that would not last.
verses.memory_phrase: Every season of ministry is precious.
verses.prayer: God, help me use well every season of opportunity You give me.
verses.discussion_question: Time for doing God's work is precious. Are you using your time well?
verses.family_discussion: This was a window of opportunity that would not last. Are there things God is calling our family to do now — while we have the chance?
verses.do_it_today: Seize one opportunity to do something good today — before it passes.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, think of one good thing you have been putting off. Set a date and do it this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures working by the river in golden afternoon light. A shadow falling in the distance — a reminder that time is limited. Poignant and purposeful.

verses.kjv: Then there arose a question between some of John's disciples and the Jews about purifying.
verses.little_bible: Some of John's disciples got into a debate with Jewish people about purification — ceremonial washing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John's disciples were having a debate with some Jewish people about washing and being clean.
verses.meaning: The debate about religious washing was common. This created the context for John's disciples to raise their concern about Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Debates about religion can distract from the real point.
verses.prayer: God, keep me from getting distracted by arguments. Help me stay focused on what matters most.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen people argue about religious rules and miss the bigger point? What should they have been focusing on?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes Christians argue about things that are not the main thing. What is the main thing we should never lose sight of?
verses.do_it_today: If you find yourself in a religious debate today, pause and ask: 'What is the main thing here?'
verses.action_challenge: Together, identify one thing that is truly at the heart of the Christian faith — and commit to keeping that central in your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of men in heated discussion, pointing and gesturing. In the background, a river where baptisms are happening — the real work continuing uninterrupted.

verses.kjv: And they came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all men come to him.
verses.little_bible: John's disciples came to him concerned: 'Teacher! The man You testified about — on the other side of the Jordan — He is also baptising now, and everyone is going to Him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John's disciples said: 'Everyone is going to Jesus now instead of to you!'
verses.meaning: John's disciples were troubled by Jesus' growing popularity. They were loyal to John — but they had missed the point. John never wanted a following for himself.
verses.memory_phrase: Success is not about the crowd — it is about faithfulness to your calling.
verses.prayer: God, help me to be faithful to my calling, not jealous of others who seem more popular.
verses.discussion_question: John's disciples were worried that Jesus was getting more popular. Why do you think that bothered them?
verses.family_discussion: Do we ever feel jealous when God blesses someone else more visibly? What is the right response?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate someone else's success today — and mean it.
verses.action_challenge: Talk about someone in your life who God is using in a visible way. Pray for them — with genuine joy, not jealousy.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of John's disciples looking worried, pointing toward a large crowd in the distance gathering around Jesus. John listening calmly, not threatened.

verses.kjv: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
verses.little_bible: John answered: 'A person cannot receive anything unless it has been given to them from heaven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'No one gets anything except what God gives them.'
verses.meaning: John was completely at peace. He knew that every good thing — influence, calling, fruit — comes from God alone. There is nothing to be jealous of.
verses.memory_phrase: Everything good comes from God.
verses.prayer: God, help me to hold everything You have given me with open hands — and rejoice when You give more to others.
verses.discussion_question: John said everything comes from heaven. What does that do to jealousy?
verses.family_discussion: If every good thing we have — talents, opportunities, blessings — comes from God, how should we hold them?
verses.do_it_today: List three things God has given you. Thank Him for each one with genuine gratitude.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, have a 'gratitude for God's gifts' conversation — each person shares one gift they are thankful for this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: John with open, outstretched hands — giving something back to the sky. A posture of release and contentment. Peace on his face.

verses.kjv: Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'You yourselves heard me say — I am not the Christ. I am only the one who was sent ahead of Him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I am not the Christ. I was just sent before Him.'
verses.meaning: John knew exactly who he was and who he was not. His identity was secure in his calling — as the forerunner, not the main event.
verses.memory_phrase: John knew his role and was at peace with it.
verses.prayer: God, help me to know who I am and who I am not — and to be at peace with the role You have given me.
verses.discussion_question: John was not the most important person — Jesus was. But John's role was still vital. Can you be content with a supporting role?
verses.family_discussion: In God's kingdom, everyone has a role. What is your family's specific role — and can you be content with it?
verses.do_it_today: Do your role well today — not someone else's. Be fully yourself in what God has called you to.
verses.action_challenge: Each family member: describe in one sentence what you think your specific role in God's plan is. Celebrate each person's unique calling.
verses.illustration_prompt: John pointing decisively toward a distant figure of Jesus, then pointing to himself with a simple, contented expression. Clear identity. No confusion.

verses.kjv: He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom's voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'The groom belongs to the bride. But the best friend of the groom stands nearby and is overjoyed when he hears the groom's voice. That is exactly how I feel — my joy is complete!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I am like the best man at a wedding — I am so happy that Jesus is here! My joy is full!'
verses.meaning: John used a wedding picture. He is the best man — his role is to celebrate the groom, not compete with him. And he does it with full, overflowing joy.
verses.memory_phrase: John's joy was full because Jesus was there.
verses.prayer: Jesus, let my greatest joy be Your presence and Your glory — not my own success.
verses.discussion_question: The best man at a wedding is not jealous of the groom — he is happy for him. How can you be truly happy when God blesses others?
verses.family_discussion: John's joy was not about his own importance — it was about Jesus being glorified. What fills your family with the deepest joy?
verses.do_it_today: Find one thing about Jesus that makes you genuinely happy and celebrate it today.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, have a 'Jesus moments' conversation: share one thing about Jesus — who He is, what He does — that fills you with joy.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful man in celebration clothes standing slightly to the side of a radiant bridegroom, laughing and clapping. Pure, unselfish joy. A wedding atmosphere.

verses.kjv: He must increase, but I must decrease.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'He must become greater, and I must become less.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'Jesus must become greater. I must become smaller.'
verses.meaning: This is one of the most profound statements in Scripture — and one of the most counter-cultural. Greatness in God's kingdom means making less of yourself and more of Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: He must increase. I must decrease.
verses.prayer: Jesus, more of You. Less of me. May Your name be great, not mine.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean for Jesus to 'increase' in your life? What does it mean for you to 'decrease'?
verses.family_discussion: This is John's life motto: less of me, more of Jesus. What would change in our family if this were our motto too?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that lifts up Jesus — and one thing that lets go of your own need for recognition.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, pray together: 'Jesus, increase in our home. We decrease. Make much of Your name here.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures: one growing brighter and taller in golden light, one bowing lower with a humble smile. The first is Jesus. The second is John — and he is at peace.

verses.kjv: He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.
verses.little_bible: The One who comes from above — from heaven — is above everything. People who come from earth speak about earthly things. But the One who comes from heaven is above everything.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus comes from heaven. That makes Him above everything.
verses.meaning: John was making the same point as Jesus in v13 — Jesus' heavenly origin gives Him supreme authority. What He says matters more than anything anyone on earth says.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus comes from above — He is above all.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are above every voice, every authority, every opinion. Help me to listen to You above everything else.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus is 'above all,' what should that mean for how we treat His words?
verses.family_discussion: In a world full of voices — social media, teachers, friends, news — how do we put Jesus' voice above all of them?
verses.do_it_today: When you hear a big opinion today, check it against what Jesus says.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, pick one topic this week — a big question about life — and look at it first through the lens of what Jesus says, not what the world says.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure descending from above a vast starry sky, radiating light. Below, the earth and all its voices are visible — small in comparison. Above all.

verses.kjv: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
verses.little_bible: Jesus testifies to what He has seen and heard — but most people do not accept what He says.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus tells what He has seen and heard — but many people did not believe Him.
verses.meaning: The tragedy of rejection: the One with perfect, firsthand knowledge of heavenly truth is rejected by those who most need it.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus testifies to truth. Will we receive it?
verses.prayer: Jesus, I receive Your testimony. I believe what You say is true.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think many people do not receive what Jesus says, even when He speaks the truth?
verses.family_discussion: Is there anything Jesus has said that has been hard for our family to receive? Let's talk about it honestly.
verses.do_it_today: Read one hard teaching of Jesus today. Ask: 'Am I willing to receive this?'
verses.action_challenge: Find one teaching of Jesus that challenges your family. Sit with it for a week — asking God to help you receive it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with open hands, a crowd turning away. A few remaining, leaning in to listen. The contrast of rejection and reception.

verses.kjv: He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.
verses.little_bible: But whoever receives what Jesus says has confirmed this truth: God is true.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Anyone who believes Jesus has confirmed: God is true.
verses.meaning: Receiving Jesus' testimony is not just personal belief — it is a declaration that God is true. Our faith is a statement about God's character.
verses.memory_phrase: Believing Jesus means trusting that God is true.
verses.prayer: God, I believe You. I trust that everything You say is true and everything You do is good.
verses.discussion_question: When you believe what Jesus says, you are saying that God is true. What does it mean for God to be true?
verses.family_discussion: Our faith is not just private — it is a declaration about God. When our family believes God, what are we saying to the world?
verses.do_it_today: Let your faith today be a statement: 'I believe Jesus. God is true.'
verses.action_challenge: As a family, write down five things you believe to be true about God. Read them out loud as a declaration.
verses.illustration_prompt: A hand pressing a golden seal onto a document — the document reads 'God is True.' A gesture of conviction and public declaration.

verses.kjv: For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
verses.little_bible: The One whom God sent speaks God's very own words — because God gives Him the Holy Spirit without any limit.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus speaks God's words because God gave Him the Holy Spirit without limit.
verses.meaning: Other prophets received the Spirit in measure — for a task, at a time. Jesus received the Spirit without limit. His words are fully, completely God's words.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus has the Spirit without measure.
verses.prayer: God, fill me with Your Spirit. And help me to treasure the words Jesus speaks.
verses.discussion_question: God gave Jesus the Spirit without measure — unlimited. What does that tell us about Jesus' words?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus speaks with the full Spirit of God. How does that change how we read the Gospels?
verses.do_it_today: Read one red-letter statement of Jesus today — a direct quote — and receive it as if God Himself is speaking directly to you.
verses.action_challenge: Pick one chapter from the Gospels this week. Read only what Jesus says. Discuss what God is saying to your family through His words.
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure overflowing with golden light — the Spirit without measure. No container, no limit. The light spills in all directions. Jesus speaking and the words themselves glow.

verses.kjv: The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into his hand.
verses.little_bible: The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in His hands.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God the Father loves Jesus and has given Him everything.
verses.meaning: The Father's love for the Son is complete, and expresses itself in total delegation of authority. All things are in Jesus' hands — including your future.
verses.memory_phrase: The Father loves the Son and gives Him everything.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You love Jesus and that everything is in His hands — including my life.
verses.discussion_question: The Father put everything in the Son's hands. What does it mean for you that your life is in Jesus' hands?
verses.family_discussion: Everything is in Jesus' hands. How does that change how we face uncertainty, fear, or the future?
verses.do_it_today: Place whatever you are worried about today consciously into Jesus' hands. Picture yourself handing it to Him.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, each person name one thing they want to place into Jesus' hands this week — and pray for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two hands — the Father's — placing a glowing orb representing the world into the open, steady hands of Jesus. Love and authority in the transfer. Warm, golden.

verses.kjv: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
verses.little_bible: Whoever believes in the Son has everlasting life right now. But whoever refuses to believe in the Son will not see life — the judgment of God remains on them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If you believe in Jesus, you have eternal life. If you do not believe, you do not have life.
verses.meaning: The chapter closes with the same note it began — new life is available only through Jesus. There is no middle ground. This is the most serious and the most wonderful truth in all the world.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in the Son — and have everlasting life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. I choose life. Thank You for the everlasting life that is mine in You.
verses.discussion_question: The verse says that those who believe have everlasting life right now — not just later. What does that mean for you today?
verses.family_discussion: This verse ends with a choice every person must make. Has every person in our family made that choice? Is there someone we love who has not yet?
verses.do_it_today: If you have not yet believed in Jesus — today is the day. If you have — celebrate it. Say: 'I have everlasting life.'
verses.action_challenge: Pray as a family for one person you love who does not yet believe in the Son. Commit to sharing the message of John 3:16-36 with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A fork in a road: one side leads toward a glowing eternal city in the distance; the other side leads into deepening shadow. A figure standing at the fork, making their choice. Solemn and hopeful.
